intro: Crucihimalaya lasiocarpa, also known as Himalayan toothpick, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glucosinolates, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various Ayurvedic formulations to support overall health and well-being.
